name: Muromonab
description: Murine monoclonal antibody specific to CD3 T-cell lymphocyte antigens. More specifically it is a purified murine (mouse) monoclonal antibody, directed against the CD3 (T3) receptor on the surface of human T-cells (T-lymphocytes) cultured using the murine ascites method. Muromonab is 93% monomeric immune globulin G type 2a (IgG2a).
indication: For treatment of organ transplant recipients, prevention of organ rejection
mechanismOfAction: Muromonab binds to the T-cell surface glycoprotein CD3 epsilon chain. It appears to kill CD-3 positive cells by inducing Fc mediated apoptosis, antibody mediated cytotoxicity and complement-dependent cytotoxicity.
pharmacodynamics: Used in organ transplant prophylaxis, Muromonab or OKT-3 binds specifically to the CD-3 complex, which is involved in antigen recognition and cell stimulation, on the surface of T lymphocytes. Immediately after administration CD-3-positive T lymphocytes are abruptly removed from circulation. It has been effective in reversing corticosteroid-resistant acute rejection in renal, liver, and cardiac transplant recipients.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Muromonab is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with Muromonab.

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: Muromonab may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with Muromonab.

interactions.name: Belimumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Muromonab is combined with Belimumab.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Muromonab.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Muromonab is combined with Leflunomide.

interactions.name: Roflumilast
